text:
Camilla Hui Guo Rou (カミーラ＝ホイコーロ, Kamīra Hoikōro) is the Second Prince of the Kakin Empire's fourteen Princes, first of Kakin's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou and his second wife, Second Queen Duazul Hui Guo Rou's two daughters and two sons. She has one younger full sister and two younger full brothers; her younger sister is 5 th Prince Tubeppa Hui Guo Rou, her older younger brother is 7 th Prince Luzurus Hui Guo Rou, and her younger younger brother is 9 th Prince Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 who was formerly a biological half-brother until his adoption by Duazul. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Appearance

Camilla is a beautiful woman with long, blond hair worn intricately, with some locks falling on her back, others tied above the nape of her neck, and four framing her face. She has large, clear black eyes and long eyelashes. 

In general, Camilla is seen wearing fancy dresses. In her first appearance, she is seen in a necked dress while also wearing a tiara. During the departure ceremony on the mainland, she wears a mermaid dress and uses an intricate hairband, and changes into a tea-length ball gown with both a choker necklace and a chandelier necklace for the portion of the ceremony taking place onboard the Black Whale. While dining with her mother after the ceremony, she wears an intricate black turtleneck. Her hairstyle varies between appearances but generally is kept in some form of an elegant updo when appearing in public. When she sets out to attack Benjamin, she wears a simple dress with a bolero jacket and black over-the-knee boots. Her hair is not styled and hangs down to her waist. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 During the trial, she wears a cropped jacket over a light-colored dress with dark accents and an interlaced design on the sides. Most of her hair is tied in a simple ponytail. 

Personality

Camilla appears to maintain charming and elegant mannerisms while interacting with the public, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 but in contrast to her public appearance, she is a cruel woman who displays no hints of emotion when considering the elimination of her siblings, just like her elder brother. She is seen ordering her mother to assassinate her youngest brother. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 According to Shimano, Camilla only listens to the King. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 

Like one might expect of the daughter of a king, Camilla has expensive tastes. She has been seen asking her mother to purchase entire series of branded, expensive, and limited edition items for her, and has been seen wanting to look through items before buying them, indicating a consideration for aesthetics instead of simply just collection. Her sister Tubeppa feels that she is repulsively greedy and that Camilla should be purged from the contest for it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 Despite her desire for beautiful clothing, she does not value them highly and has been seen leaving items of clothing strewn on the floor instead of putting them away properly. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 

Camilla is self-centered and has not shown respect for anyone other than her father. She has been seen ordering her mother to kill her brother Halkenburg, and has called her older brother Benjamin stupid in front of their father. When addressing her father, she infantilizes herself by referring to herself as "Cammy", which could be interpreted as her hoping that her father would bend the succession contest in her favor. Her general self-centeredness is also seen from her wanting everyone to act as she desires, and her perception of the world as unfair simply because her wishing for something is insufficient to make it true. 

Background

Camilla gave the Have-Nots of Kakin the chance to become her private guards. She had a special district built on her private property where they can enjoy privileges equal to those of the military, which earned her the support of the group. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Camilla is first officially seen when Kakin's 14 Princes are introduced. Camilla is seen with her hands clasped in a praying gesture in front of her chest, wearing an intricate dress and her hair in an elaborate updo adorned with a tiara-shaped ornament, and introduced as the second Prince, daughter of 2 nd Queen Duazul. During the eve (of departure) festival, Camilla is seen with the rest of her siblings and Beyond Netero on a stage, smiling faintly at the crowd from the side of the group. Kakin's Royal Family is flown into the Black Whale 1 shortly after, where they reside on Tier 1 during the journey to the New Continent. Camilla is next seen attending a departure ceremony taking place in the ballroom on Tier 1 of the Black Whale 1 in a different dress, next to her siblings 1 st Prince Benjamin, 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei, and 5 th Prince Tubeppa. During this departure ceremony, her Guardian Spirit Beast is seen for the first time; an elegant cnidarian-like creature without a clear head. 

The Black Whale 1 departs the next day, and its departure horn is sounded, marking the beginning of the succession contest. A departure ceremony is held in a banquet hall on Tier 1, which all Princes attend to qualify as a participant in the succession contest. Before she leaves the departure ceremony, Camilla approaches her father Nasubi to clarify the definitions of "survival" and "dropped out" for the Succession War. Nasubi informs her that Zhang Lei already asked the same question, and he would give her the same response, word for word. He tells her that "The sole survivor is the official heir to the throne", and that how she interprets it is also part of the war. Benjamin joins the conversation, having overheard part of their exchange, and tells Camilla that a king has no need to bother with how others define things. Camilla exclaims that she only wants people to do as she wants, and that her siblings should want her to win and volunteer to die on their own. Benjamin calls her conceited and unfit to the throne. She calls him stupid and antagonizes him, but their father orders her to show respect for her older brother. Instead of conceding, Camilla merely brushes them both off and walks away. Annoyed, Camilla thinks of how the world is unfair, and a head begins to emerge on her Guardian Spirit Beast. 

After Benjamin departs from the departure ceremony, he orders his men to guard the other princes, including Camilla. In response, Camilla tells her bodyguards to not let them into her sight, and to keep them outside the living room. She also asks her bodyguards to get her mother, Queen Duazul. 

Later that day, Camilla dines with her mother in Room 1002 while talking about buying clothes (from Musen, Coachpie, and Harryweb's Noctiluca line). She casually and abruptly pivots the conversation and discusses her plans to kill Benjamin first and then Halkenburg, ordering her mother to get closer to Halkenburg. When Queen Duazul expresses her misgivings, Camilla sternly orders her to follow. Unbeknownst to her, Benjamin's bodyguard Musse eavesdrops on her plans from where he was confined to in Room 1002, and plans to use his Nen ability on her. 

That night, Camilla rejects Kurapika's offer to have her Royal Bodyguards learn Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 

During the evening of the next day, Camilla sets out to kill Benjamin. On her way out, she encounters Musse and divulges her plans to kill Benjamin, giving Musse a choice to join her or die. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 Having recorded her conversations, Musse threatens her with arrest for conspiring to murder Benjamin and points a gun on her, warning her not to step closer. Camilla calls his bluff and continues to step toward Musse, making him nervous. Musse notes that Camilla is is in a state of Zetsu and surmises she must have a counter-type ability. He decides to shoot, expecting to either be able to learn her ability, or to kill her for Benjamin. He shoots and hits her thrice, once in the forehead and twice in the chest. Camilla collapses to the ground. Her men hear the commotion and rush to find her, but find the doors locked. Musse cautiously approaches her but remembers to fulfil his ability and manages to activate it right before Camilla's Cat's Name activates, consuming Musse to restore and resurrect Camilla. 

Camilla's men force a door open, see blood, and ask her what happened. Camilla lies and says that Musse had shot her but escaped, says that she is going to chase him, and orders her men to burn his personal belongings. They try to dissuade her, asking her to calm down and telling her she would play into Benjamin's hands by chasing Musse and incinerating his items. Camilla asks them if they would rather go themselves, and risk having their entire families slaughtered. They turn to her mother, Queen Duazul for help in convincing Camilla, but Duazul tells them to stay behind and let Camilla do as she wishes. 

Camilla's men watch her as she turns around the corner in the corridor outside her room, and see Furykov and another guard step out to stop her. The two see the gun in her hand and raise their own guns at her, calling out to her to stop. Benjamin listens through Furykov's device and tells Furykov to not attack and let her through. Camilla aims at the heads of Benjamin's guards and shoots. Furykov is able to react and guard himself with Ken, but the other is killed. Camilla expresses her surprise to him and tries to goad him into retaliating, but Furykov restrains himself and follows her into Room 1001. 

Camilla and Benjamin have a short exchange, and Camilla demands Benjamin turn over Musse. Benjamin tells her he refuses to play along with her charade, and calls for her arrest. Camilla opens fire and empties her magazine at Benjamin, also hitting Balsamilco standing nearby, but Benjamin and Balsamilco simply guard with Ken and Furykov snaps her arm when she runs out of bullets. Camilla breaks into a sweat and threatens to torture and then kill Furykov's family, but Furykov is not threatened by this, and Camilla is placed into a holding cell, watched by Balsamilco Might and Furykov. Furykov suggests calling one of Benjamin's bodyguards who can use Nen back to observe her and her Guardian Spirit Beast in the cell, but Balsamilco explains that Musse managed to activate his Secret Window, allowing them to observe her. 

The next day, Benjamin (represented by Balsamilco) and Camilla (represented by one of her soldiers) stand trial before Supreme Magistrate Cleapatro after Benjamin accuses Camilla of killing Musse. Camilla's representative claims self-defense from Musse's attempted assassination and a fear for her life when seeing Wolfe and Furykov point guns at her, and files a countersuit against Benjamin on suspicion of ordering her assassination. Balsamilco objects and points out that the number of shots Camilla fired was excessive. Both sides request the others' quarters are searched for Musse. Cleapatro puts in a request for the Royal Army to do so, and has both princes confined to the VVIP area and put under surveillance. Cleapatro allows them to keep their guards but tells them that a court-assigned escort would accompany them on any outings before adjourning the court. Benjamin and Balsamilco watch as Camilla leaves with two of her guards. Balsamilco remarks about their misfortune in Camilla escaping confinement, but Benjamin deems it a non-issue since Musse's Secret Window lets them see her actions. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

During the first Sunday banquet on the eighth day, Camilla is briefly transfixed by the music of Melody's flute performance, despite hearing it through a speaker system instead of in person. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383 

On the ninth day of the voyage, Camilla sits and looks at her reflection in a mirror in her residence in the VVIP area while under surveillance as her servants tidy the room and make her bed around her. While standing guard, Moswana looks at a picture of Benjamin and mentally reinforces her desire to curse Benjamin for Camilla. Sarahell has a briefing with Camilla's men, discussing the possible reasons behind Shikaku's actions and the possibility Halkenburg's men use curses like them. Sarahell reminds Umanma to put her life on the line to exorcise any curse Camilla receives. She also instructs Nukuocon and Cavic to use their lives for Camilla and the future, reminding them that Camilla gave them a choice while they were still social outcasts. Sarahell tells Camilla's men to investigate the presence of exorcists among the other princes should their targets die, and ends the meeting by reminding them not to bother Camilla. 

Later, Sarahell speaks with Futataki about testing their offensive capabilities from a distance. After Futataki advises her that proximity is key to making an effective curse, Sarahell decides to go to the next set of Nen classes that Kurapika hosts despite Camilla not wanting them to ask for Nen lessons, so that she can test out her curse on Woble, likely the weakest of curses that Camilla's men can perform. 

At 11:45 a.m. on the tenth day of the voyage, Kurapika and Bill invite Longhi into Room 1014's master bedroom in order to privately initiate her with Nen, as they did with her unawakened classmates. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 Longhi declines the divination as she reveals that she can use Nen and asks Kurapika for his help. She reveals her identity as Beyond's child, and shares that she had learnt of it when her half-sister Makaha had discovered her after she began her own investigation as a result of her parent's uncharacteristically blasé reaction to her not being selected to be Camilla's personal guard, since Camilla had directly selected the Have-Nots for that position. She asks for Kurapika's help in investigating her theory that Beyond is the father of one of the 14 Princes, and contracts the terms of Kurapika's assignment and agreement on top of his truce with Tubeppa using her Moonlight Act. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 401 

Between 6:00 a.m. and 8:50 a.m. on the eleventh day of the voyage, 11 th Prince Fugetsu is escorted around Tier 1 by Kaiser and the Ministry of Justice to deliver a letter that the recently-deceased 10 th Prince Kacho had written for each Prince, to be delivered in case of her death. Camilla receives a letter, and reads it. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Later that day, Camilla is seen having the nails on her right hand painted by a servant as she lounges in a chair, drink in the other hand, watching something on a tablet. Nearby, Fukataki directs other servants to tidy things up. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

Around the same time, Kurapika prepares to receive Camilla's guard for the second round of Nen lessons, beginning the next day. He asks Sakata and Hashito why they thought she changed her mind about sending her men to the lessons, and Sakata suggests that Camilla had been waiting to verify or wanted to verify his claims, pointing out that there must be some benefit for her side if Camilla had changed her mind about her guards attending. Hashito says that there could be any reason for it, genuine or malicious. To the side, Babimyna thinks about the rumors that her guards use unique curses, and wonders if they were Nen abilities, and if Woble was the target. Kurapika interprets Camilla sending only one person shows firm resolve, and considering her personality, convinces himself that they had ulterior motives, and did not want to simply learn. The letter sent to him by Melody containing the Princes' secrets did not mention Camilla, and so Kurapika wonders if Camilla had a secret that forced her to send an attendee. Kurapika tells Bill to keep Camilla's guards away from Woble as much as possible. 

Camilla's Employees

Camilla Hui Guo Rou's personal army

2 nd Prince Camilla is known to have a personal army. The exact strength of her personal army is unknown, but she notably gave all the Have-Nots, a social class of outcasts, the opportunity to join her army. Fourteen of these Have-Nots are on board with Camilla, but it is unclear exactly how many of them are counted as part of her allotted fifteen employees, since at least four of them are known to be Provisional Hunters, allowed to board in exchange for contribution toward the Hunter Association's later mission on the Dark Continent, letting them assist a Prince without counting toward their allotted employees.

It is unclear if Camilla's Have-nots are also members of Kakin's military. It is unclear if Fukataki, Camilla's Majordomo, is a Have-Not or if she is part of Camilla's personal army. No greater rank structure has been disclosed, though one of them acts as their captain for the Black Whale 1's voyage.

During their time on board the Black Whale 1, Camilla's soldiers all wear dark-colored combat gear with black combat boots. Their jackets have two chest pockets, and they wear a belt over their jackets. 

The Have-Nots on board the ship are known to be Nen users. Thirteen of the fourteen confirmed Have-Nots are able to inflict a slow, Nen-based curse on a designated target. The time taken for their curse to activate varies by how long they have personally spent preparing to curse their target, but it is guaranteed to kill their target if not interfered with. The final Have-Not on board is a Nen exorcist, expected to exorcise any hostile curses. Fukataki has enough knowledge about the Have-Nots' common ability to be consulted by them. 

Equipment

Handgun: Camilla briefly wielded Musse's gun after his death. She used it as a weapon as well as a way to get the opponent to attack her in order to activate her Nen ability. The handgun was confiscated following her arrest. 

Abilities & Powers

Camilla's status as one of the senior Princes of Kakin grants her significant political influences and assets that give her benefits to win the throne. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 Camilla is confident enough in herself to the point of not fearing her elder brother, Benjamin. She is moderately intelligent, enough to use Musse's disappearance to gain entrance to Benjamin's quarters as well as to defend herself in court. She was seemingly able to hide her status as a Nen user from Benjamin until she made her move against Musse, and by elevating the Have-Nots, she secured an army of extremely loyal Nen users. 

Enhanced Endurance: Camilla withstood having her arm broken with only a slight change in expression and an increase in perspiration. 

Proficient Marksman: Camilla is a trained shooter. She is able to shoot two targets in the head in very quick succession. She is also relatively strong, able to pull the trigger of a double-action pistol several times in rapid succession. 

Nen

Camilla is a powerful Nen user. Her knowledge of post-mortem Nen allows her to employ it in her counteractive Nen ability, which makes even Benjamin hesitant about killing her. She is at least capable of using Hatsu and Zetsu. 

The fact that she was seemingly able to use her arm for mundane tasks no later than ten days after it was broken (if not the following day) might suggest a supernatural rate of recovery that is compatible with proficiency in Enhancement.

Guardian Spirit Beast

Like the rest of her siblings, Camilla received an egg from which a Guardian Spirit Beast hatched to protect her. Since it is a parasitic-type ability, she has no control over it. The Nen beast is subjected to two rule-like instincts: it is unable to fight other Guardian Spirit Beasts as well as to directly attack the host of one. It is known to be a Manipulator with a coercive-type ability that takes complete control of a target who fulfills certain conditions. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

The Guardian Spirit Beast did not manifest itself when Musse fatally shot Camilla, and it did not prevent him from using his ability on her or interact with it even days after the fact. Whether this is because Camilla was in a state of Zetsu in the first case (despite other Guardian Spirit Beasts being able to run on stored aura) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 385 and temporarily dead in the second, or for some other reason, it is currently unknown.

Manga Appearances

Battles

Quotes

(To Nasubi) "Father. Your little Cammy would like to clarify something. The word "survival" could be defined in several ways. This makes the rules for "dropping out" ambiguous. Cammy wishes that you'd not accept dropouts that are not biological death. Is that okay with you?" 
(To Benjamin) "Cammy wants everyone in the whole world to do what Cammy wants! First, Cammy wants all other princes to die! Ideally, to volunteer to die! They have to think, 'Since Camilla is going to be king, I should kill myself'!" 
(To Duazul) "Mummy... don't get the wrong idea. That was an order!" 
(To Musse) "I'm going to kill First Prince Benjamin. Either help me or die. Make your choice. Aren't I nice?" 
(To Musse) "I don't need a Guardian Spirit Beast. I have him...! Nen triggered after death makes it powerful! A resuscitation ability makes me invincible!!" 
(To Furykov) "I'll kill you and your family. Your entire clan. After I make sure to inflict unimaginable suffering...!" 

Trivia

In Chapter 389, Camilla is shown to be in a room labeled "302", in the VVIP area. In Chapter 403, Benjamin is shown to be in a room labeled "1001", in the VVIP area. Both Princes are confined to the VVIP area during this time, and it is unclear why there is a difference in room numberings.

Intertextuality and References

The name Cat's Name of Camilla's ability is likely a reference to J-Pop idol group Keyakizaka46's song 猫の名前 (Neko no Namae, lit. Cat's Name).
Cat's Name says "ネる, ねル, ネル, ね (neru, neru, neru, ne)" as it kneads Musse into life energy to revive Camilla. Neko no Namae is performed by members from Kanji Keyakizaka46 (櫸坂46) and Hiragana Keyakizaka46 (けやき坂46), the two subgroups of Keyakizaka46 referenced in a bonus page in Volume 35. Perhaps coincidentally, while (neru) does mean to knead, ねる (neru) is also the name of Nagahama Neru (ねる), the Keyakizaka46 member that was the reason for Keyakizaka46 having its two subgroups.
The alternate name of Camilla's ability, "The Cat That Lived a Million Times", is the title of a children's book by Yōko Sano.
